sub_pixel_system.notes: Flight simulator; sub-pixel precision not documented

special_mechanics.name: Flight Dynamics
special_mechanics.description: Game claimed to use flight dynamics from real military simulator. Required math coprocessor for high-fidelity flight mode. Multiple difficulty modes with varying physics complexity.

physics_values.fidelity_levels: high-fidelity mode (math coprocessor required), standard modes

special_mechanics.name: Padlock View
special_mechanics.description: Player POV slews in direction of selected target, scanning around cockpit as needed. Camera/view mechanic rather than movement mechanic.

special_mechanics.name: Aircraft Control
special_mechanics.description: F-16 Fighting Falcon flight model with cockpit controls including radar and SCP (Situational Control Panel). Multiple aircraft variants available through expansions.

physics_values.aircraft_variant: F-16A (base), F-16 FSX variant (expansion), MiG-29 (expansion), F/A-18 Hornet (expansion)

tier_notes: Tier 2 (T2) - Empirical/qualitative data only. Source is Wikipedia article focused on game history, features, and reception rather than technical physics documentation. No numerical physics parameters documented in available source. Game is a flight simulator, not a platformer, so standard movement mechanics (walk, jump, gravity) do not apply. Flight dynamics described qualitatively as realistic and derived from military simulators.
